subject: Databases
title: SQL in the Real World
description: A short lesson to help you apply SQL in the Real World.

hook.text: Data analysts write SQL daily. So do backend developers, DBAs, and data scientists. It's universal.

buildup.text: Business intelligence tools (Metabase, Looker) generate SQL under the hood. Know SQL and you know the engine.

discovery.text: Even NoSQL databases added SQL-like query languages. Cassandra has CQL, Spark has SparkSQL.

twist.text: AI can generate SQL from natural language, but reviewing and fixing it still requires understanding SQL.

climax.text: SQL is a career multiplier. It's useful in engineering, analytics, product, and data science roles.

punchline.text: SQL: one skill, every data role. Learn it well.

quiz.question: Why is SQL knowledge still valuable even with AI-generated queries?
options: You need to review, understand, and fix the SQL that AI produces
AI cannot generate any SQL at all
SQL will be completely replaced by AI within a year
